books.title: Phineas Finn
books.author: Anthony Trollope
books.summary: A young Irishman attempts to establish himself as a member of Parliament while finding his way in high society.

books.title: The Last of the Mohicans
books.author: James Fenimore Cooper
books.summary: Frontiersmen and Native Americans help transport a colonel’s daughters through upper New York during the French and Indian War.

books.title: The Autobiography of Calvin Coolidge
books.author: Calvin Coolidge
books.summary: The thirtieth president of the United States recounts his life story.

books.title: Recollections of Full Years
books.author: Helen Herron Taft
books.summary: Helen Herron Taft, wife of William Howard Taft, the twenty-seventh President of the United States, recalls her life married to one of America’s most influential political figures.

books.title: Black Beauty
books.author: Anna Sewell
books.summary: A memoir narrated by a horse whose idyllic time on an English farm prepares him for a life filled with constant change and varied circumstances.

books.title: Back to Methuselah
books.author: George Bernard Shaw
books.summary: Five linked plays explore human longevity and governance.

books.title: Edward III
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: Edward III declares himself the true heir of France, which launches the Hundred Years’ War.

books.title: The Road to Oz
books.author: L. Frank Baum
books.summary: A young girl takes a passerby down the road near her farm when they find themselves transported to another land.

books.title: The Pothunters
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: The theft of sports trophies from an English public school has unexpected implications on the lives of the school’s students and masters.

books.title: The Portent
books.author: George MacDonald
books.summary: A young Scottish tutor, gifted with a supernatural sense, discovers a haunting connection to a somnambulant noblewoman while residing at a remote English estate.

books.title: Roughing It
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: Mark Twain’s semiautobiographical account of his travel across the United States.

books.title: Kate Plus 10
books.author: Edgar Wallace
books.summary: A police inspector investigates an enigmatic young woman and her connection with various criminal activities.

books.title: A Week on the Concord and Merrimack Rivers
books.author: Henry David Thoreau
books.summary: On a rowboat excursion with his brother, Thoreau lets his thoughts meander with the river.

books.title: A Voice from the South
books.author: Anna Julia Cooper
books.summary: A Black woman from the American South philosophizes on various subjects.

books.title: The Canterbury Tales
books.author: Geoffrey Chaucer
books.summary: A group of pilgrims trade tales as they travel through medieval England.

books.title: Black No More
books.author: George Schuyler
books.summary: A medical process that makes black people look white revolutionizes American society.

books.title: The Last Man
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.summary: Love and war in future Europe are thrown into confusion by a global pandemic that obliterates humanity.

books.title: Irish Fairy Tales
books.author: James Stephens
books.summary: Ten stories from the Fenian cycle of Irish folklore retold by James Stephens.

books.title: The Pilgrim’s Progress
books.author: John Bunyan
books.summary: An allegorical story of a pilgrim in search of redemption, followed later by his wife and children, passing through great trials before reaching the Celestial City.

books.title: Journals
books.author: Alexander Mackenzie
books.summary: Explorer Alexander Mackenzie details his journeys to both the Arctic and Pacific Oceans in his personal journals.
